stage.name: CV32A60X profile: fix one learning target
stage.description: A profile lesson that explains why the English CVA6 public course uses CV32A60X as the first concrete configuration target.
stage.courseTemplateName: Professional Technical Deep Dive - Evidence-Grounded SlideKit

scenes.title: Fix one target before reading deeply

content.title: CV32A60X profile: fix one learning target
content.subtitle: A public micro-course needs one concrete configuration before it teaches pipeline and module behavior.
content.thesis: Choosing CV32A60X first is not narrowing ambition. It is making the first technical path inspectable.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X Design Document objective
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The CV32A60X design document describes a specific configuration and provides information for RTL modification and RTL verification rather than software-only instruction lookup.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 initial release configuration table
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The requirements table identifies CV32A60X as an ASIC IMC 32-bit configuration with no FPU, CV-X-IF support, no MMU, and small L1 I/D caches.

content.speakerNotes: Now we choose a first concrete target: CV32A60X. This does not mean the rest of CVA6 is unimportant. It means the public course needs one configuration where evidence, slides, narration, and quiz questions line up. Without a named target, every architecture statement becomes either too vague or too broad.

actions.text: Now we choose a first concrete target: CV32A60X. This does not mean the rest of CVA6 is unimportant. It means the public course needs one configuration where evidence, slides, narration, and quiz questions line up. Without a named target, every architecture statement becomes either too vague or too broad.

scenes.title: The CV32A60X profile in one pass

content.title: CV32A60X gives us a concrete starting row
content.subtitle: The requirements and User Manual tables make the first learning target explicit.

cards.title: ISA shape
cards.body: IMC-style baseline in the requirements release table, with CV-X-IF support called out.

cards.title: Privilege and MMU
cards.body: Machine-only in the User Manual configuration row; no MMU in the release table.

cards.title: Cache size
cards.body: Small L1 instruction and data cache values in the initial release table.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 initial release configuration table
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The requirements table identifies CV32A60X as an ASIC IMC 32-bit configuration with no FPU, CV-X-IF support, no MMU, and small L1 I/D caches.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 configuration family table
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The User Manual table compares CV32A60AX, CV32A60X, and CV64A6_MMU by target, privilege levels, ISA, and CV-X-IF support.

content.speakerNotes: The profile snapshot is intentionally plain. CV32A60X is a thirty-two-bit embedded core context, with an ASIC target, CV-X-IF support, Machine-only privilege in the User Manual row, no MMU in the release table, and small L1 cache values. These details are not random facts. They are the boundaries for the next wave of lessons.

actions.text: The profile snapshot is intentionally plain. CV32A60X is a thirty-two-bit embedded core context, with an ASIC target, CV-X-IF support, Machine-only privilege in the User Manual row, no MMU in the release table, and small L1 cache values. These details are not random facts. They are the boundaries for the next wave of lessons.

scenes.title: What the CV32A60X design document is for

content.title: Use it for RTL-oriented design understanding
content.subtitle: The document tells us what kind of depth it is trying to support.

items.title: Specific configuration
items.body: It describes CV32A60X, not every possible CVA6 variant.

items.title: RTL modification
items.body: It is meant to provide enough context for modifying RTL.

items.title: RTL verification
items.body: It supports verification-oriented understanding of behavior and modules.

items.title: Module framework
items.body: Sections are organized around description, functionality, architecture, modules, and registers.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X Design Document objective
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The CV32A60X design document describes a specific configuration and provides information for RTL modification and RTL verification rather than software-only instruction lookup.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design documentation framework
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The CV32A60X design document organizes modules with description, functionality, architecture/modules, and register sections where applicable.

content.speakerNotes: The CV32A60X design document tells us its purpose. It describes a specific configuration and aims to give enough information for RTL modification and RTL verification. That is exactly the source we want for serious lessons. It is also why we should not use it to make casual statements about every future CVA6 configuration.

actions.text: The CV32A60X design document tells us its purpose. It describes a specific configuration and aims to give enough information for RTL modification and RTL verification. That is exactly the source we want for serious lessons. It is also why we should not use it to make casual statements about every future CVA6 configuration.

content.title: CV32A60X gives us a named pipeline route
content.subtitle: The design document describes the high-level subsystem behavior before the module chapters.

steps.title: Instruction fetch
steps.body: Bring instructions into the frontend path.

steps.title: Decode and issue
steps.body: Decode instruction meaning, then issue toward execution.

steps.title: Execute and commit
steps.body: Execute work and retire results in order.

content.takeaway: We will return to each stage later. For now, the target is to know the route exists.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X subsystem global functionality
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The CV32A60X subsystem is described as Harvard-based, issuing instructions in order, executing out of order, committing in order, and using six named pipeline stages.

content.speakerNotes: The design document gives a first architecture baseline. It names a Harvard-based subsystem, in-order issue, out-of-order execution, in-order commit, and a six-stage flow from PC generation through commit. We are not unpacking every stage yet. We are fixing the route so later lessons can zoom into one part without losing the map.

actions.text: The design document gives a first architecture baseline. It names a Harvard-based subsystem, in-order issue, out-of-order execution, in-order commit, and a six-stage flow from PC generation through commit. We are not unpacking every stage yet. We are fixing the route so later lessons can zoom into one part without losing the map.

scenes.title: Why not teach every variant at once?

content.title: Depth needs a first anchor
content.subtitle: A micro-course can mention the family, but it should teach depth through a named configuration.

columns.title: Family awareness
columns.body: Learners should know that CVA6 supports multiple configurations and future products.

columns.title: Profile depth
columns.body: Learners need one concrete profile where module, parameter, and quiz claims are source-backed.

content.takeaway: We can broaden later because we are precise now.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 project family goal
sourceAnchors.locator: overview/index.md CVA6 application class RISC-V CPU core
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The documentation overview states that CVA6 is a family of production-quality, open-source, application-class RISC-V CPU cores targeting ASIC and FPGA implementations.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X Design Document objective
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The CV32A60X design document describes a specific configuration and provides information for RTL modification and RTL verification rather than software-only instruction lookup.

content.speakerNotes: There is a course-design reason for this. If we teach every variant at once, the first course becomes a catalog. If we teach only one variant without saying so, we overclaim. The better path is family awareness plus profile depth. We mention the family, then use CV32A60X as the first anchor. We can broaden later because we are precise now.

actions.text: There is a course-design reason for this. If we teach every variant at once, the first course becomes a catalog. If we teach only one variant without saying so, we overclaim. The better path is family awareness plus profile depth. We mention the family, then use CV32A60X as the first anchor. We can broaden later because we are precise now.

scenes.title: What not to generalize from CV32A60X

content.title: A profile is useful because it has limits
content.subtitle: Mark these limits now so later architecture lessons stay honest.

items.title: Width
items.body: A 32-bit profile should not be silently treated as a 64-bit profile.

items.title: Privilege
items.body: Machine-only behavior should not be generalized to configurations with Supervisor and User levels.

items.title: MMU and FPU
items.body: Absent optional features should not be taught as universal CVA6 facts.

items.title: Pipeline-only top
items.body: Parameter values can change which top-level structure a lesson is really describing.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 configuration family table
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The User Manual table compares CV32A60AX, CV32A60X, and CV64A6_MMU by target, privilege levels, ISA, and CV-X-IF support.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X parameter configuration table
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The CV32A60X parameter table records values such as XLEN 32, VLEN 32, RVC true, MmuPresent false, CvxifEn true, PipelineOnly true, and single commit port behavior.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 optional feature parameter rule
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The requirements specification states that optional features are controlled by SystemVerilog parameters and are absent from the synthesized netlist when not selected.

content.speakerNotes: A profile is valuable because it has limits. CV32A60X lets us be concrete, but we must not generalize its thirty-two-bit profile, Machine-only privilege, no-MMU release context, or parameter choices to every configuration. The goal is not to hide that complexity. The goal is to introduce it in the right order.

actions.text: A profile is valuable because it has limits. CV32A60X lets us be concrete, but we must not generalize its thirty-two-bit profile, Machine-only privilege, no-MMU release context, or parameter choices to every configuration. The goal is not to hide that complexity. The goal is to introduce it in the right order.

questions.question: Why use CV32A60X as the first deep learning target?

options.label: It gives a concrete configuration for source-backed technical claims
It makes every other configuration irrelevant
It removes the need for source anchors
It is only a marketing name

questions.analysis: A named configuration lets the course make precise, inspectable statements.

questions.question: What is the design document mainly useful for in this course?

options.label: RTL-oriented understanding for modification and verification
Replacing the README for repository setup

questions.analysis: The design document is aimed at RTL modification and verification understanding.

questions.question: Which items are part of the CV32A60X profile evidence used here?

options.label: No MMU in the initial release table
Guaranteed FPU for every CVA6 variant

questions.analysis: The profile evidence supports A, B, and C. FPU is not guaranteed universally.

questions.question: What is the safest way to use a CV32A60X-specific detail?

options.label: Say it applies to CV32A60X unless another source broadens it
Present it as universal CVA6 behavior immediately
Turn it into a free-form quiz answer

questions.analysis: Configuration-specific details should stay attached to the configuration unless broader evidence is cited.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X Design Document objective
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The CV32A60X design document describes a specific configuration and provides information for RTL modification and RTL verification rather than software-only instruction lookup.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 initial release configuration table
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The requirements table identifies CV32A60X as an ASIC IMC 32-bit configuration with no FPU, CV-X-IF support, no MMU, and small L1 I/D caches.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X subsystem global functionality
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The CV32A60X subsystem is described as Harvard-based, issuing instructions in order, executing out of order, committing in order, and using six named pipeline stages.

content.speakerNotes: This checkpoint tests whether CV32A60X is now clear as a teaching anchor. We choose it so the first technical path is concrete, not because the family has no other members. Keep that distinction in mind, and the later pipeline lessons will feel much more grounded.

actions.text: This checkpoint tests whether CV32A60X is now clear as a teaching anchor. We choose it so the first technical path is concrete, not because the family has no other members. Keep that distinction in mind, and the later pipeline lessons will feel much more grounded.

content.title: Now the course has a concrete technical baseline
content.subtitle: The next lesson turns that profile into a parameter-reading map.
takeaways: CV32A60X is a concrete learning anchor, not the entire CVA6 universe.
Its design document supports RTL-oriented teaching.
Architecture lessons should name the configuration they depend on.
Limits make the lesson more trustworthy, not less useful.
content.nextStep: Learn which CV32A60X parameters must be checked before making conclusions about width, privilege, optional features, cache, and interfaces.
content.closing: A serious course does not avoid scope. It uses scope to make depth possible.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X Design Document objective
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The CV32A60X design document describes a specific configuration and provides information for RTL modification and RTL verification rather than software-only instruction lookup.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X parameter configuration table
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The CV32A60X parameter table records values such as XLEN 32, VLEN 32, RVC true, MmuPresent false, CvxifEn true, PipelineOnly true, and single commit port behavior.

content.speakerNotes: We now have a concrete baseline. CV32A60X is the first profile we can teach deeply, with source-backed statements and local quizzes. The next lesson asks the most important configuration question: which parameters should you check before making a conclusion? Once that habit is in place, the pipeline lessons can become much more precise.

actions.text: We now have a concrete baseline. CV32A60X is the first profile we can teach deeply, with source-backed statements and local quizzes. Once that habit is in place, the pipeline lessons can become much more precise.
